name: Tehran Persian (Standard Iranian)

ancestors.notes: New Persian descent; Tehran dialect became standard ~19th c.
Arabic adstrate: 40–50% of formal vocabulary; consonants θ/ð/ħ/ʕ/q assimilated phonologically

notes: Tehran Standard Persian — the national standard of Iran. DEFINING VOWEL SHIFT (vs. Classical/Dari/Tajik): Classical ā [aː] → Tehran [ɒː] (backed); Classical a → [æ] (fronted). 6-VOWEL SYSTEM: ɒː, æ, e, o, iː, uː (no contrastive length). Voiceless stops aspirated [pʰ tʰ kʰ] in onset. q/ɣ merger: Arabic ق and غ both → [ɣ] or [ɢ]. ħ → h (pharyngeal lost); ʕ → ʔ (often deleted). r → [ɾ] tap in most positions.

sources.author: Windfuhr, G.
sources.title: Persian Grammar: History and State of Its Study
